source: Collaborative Deep Learning for Recommender Systems

concepts.finding: Collaborative Deep Learning (CDL): Bayesian deep learning framework combining stacked denoising autoencoders (SDAE) with PMF for content+ratings. Item content (text) feeds into SDAE, latent representation connects to PMF item latent vector. Jointly optimizes content reconstruction and rating prediction. Outperforms pure PMF and pure content-based recommendation.
concepts.implication: Joint model of keyword content (text features via autoencoder) and keyword interaction context (co-occurrence via embedding). Content autoencoder regularizes co-occurrence embeddings: keywords with similar text have similar embeddings, smoothed by co-occurrence.
concepts.open_question: What is the optimal balance between keyword content (text features) and context (co-occurrence) in joint models like CDL?
concepts.testable_hypothesis: A CDL-style joint model (autoencoder for keyword text + embedding for keyword co-occurrence) produces better intent clusters than content-only or co-occurrence-only embeddings.
